title: Aureline icon-slot and illustration-slot map record
description: Boundary schema for the icon_slot_map_record frozen in docs/ux/theme_and_visual_asset_contract.md. Every icon slot or illustration slot a component contract, theme package, or extension overlay points at resolves through one record of this shape. The record names the slot id, slot kind (canonical command icon, status semantic, trust state, file type, directional chevron/arrow, illustration anchor, illustration empty-state, illustration onboarding), the canonical metaphor ref, the platform-variant coverage matrix, the directionality / RTL behavior, the safety-critical metaphor-continuity classification, the migration / supersession state, the extension or theme override boundary, and the typed evidence hooks that prove the slot keeps its meaning across replacements. The schema mints no parallel theme_class, accessibility_posture_class, icon_treatment_class, semantic_status_class, trust_visual_state_class, or deployment_profile_id vocabulary; those values are re-exported by reference. Adding a new icon_slot_kind_class, icon_directionality_class, safety_critical_class, platform_variant_class, extension_override_boundary_class, migration_state_class, or denial_reason is additive-minor and bumps theme_asset_schema_version; repurposing an existing value is breaking and requires a new decision row. Raw asset bytes, raw rendered bitmaps, raw URLs, and raw absolute paths never cross this boundary; this schema carries opaque ids, typed vocabulary, and short privacy-safe labels only.

opaque_id.description: Opaque, stable id safe to log, safe on RPC, safe in manifests / support bundles. Raw URLs, raw absolute paths, raw asset bytes never appear here.

monotonic_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp produced from a monotonic clock source.

icon_slot_kind_class.description: Closed icon-slot / illustration-slot kind vocabulary. command_canonical pins one canonical command metaphor; status_semantic pairs with semantic_status_class; trust_state pairs with trust_visual_state_class; file_type covers file-type metaphors; directional_chevron / directional_arrow are mirrorable directional glyphs; illustration_* slots are reserved for onboarding, empty states, and documentation. See contract §7.

icon_directionality_class.description: Closed directionality vocabulary. mirror_in_rtl flips the glyph horizontally for RTL locales (typical for chevrons / arrows / progress / read-direction); literal_no_mirror keeps the glyph identical (typical for command_canonical, file_type, and brand marks); not_applicable is reserved for slots that carry no directional metaphor (most status_semantic / trust_state slots and most illustrations). See contract §7.

safety_critical_class.description: Closed safety-critical classification. A safety-critical slot's metaphor MAY NOT change semantics across replacement, theme override, or extension contribution without explicit migration metadata; a non-safety-critical slot may evolve under ordinary lifecycle rules. See contract §7.

platform_variant_class.description: Closed platform-variant vocabulary. A slot MAY ship the same glyph across all platforms (not_applicable_universal) or MAY ship a per-platform variant where native conventions diverge; missing variants are declared coverage gaps rather than silent fallbacks. See contract §7.

extension_override_boundary_class.description: Closed extension-override boundary vocabulary. host_owned_no_override locks the slot to the first-party metaphor; extension_may_replace_with_disclosure permits replacement only with a declared migration record; extension_may_extend_only allows extensions to add adjacent variants without replacing the canonical metaphor; not_extension_consumable reserves the slot entirely (e.g. trust_state slots backing security-critical chrome). See contract §7.

theme_override_boundary_class.description: Closed theme-override boundary vocabulary. theme_may_recolor_only allows themes to retint the glyph through icon_treatment_class without changing the metaphor; theme_may_replace_glyph_with_disclosure permits glyph replacement only with a declared migration record; theme_locked_to_first_party_glyph forbids any theme glyph swap (typical for safety-critical and trust_state slots). See contract §7.

migration_state_class.description: Closed lifecycle vocabulary for icon / illustration slots. frozen is the steady state; migrating_with_alias means a new slot id has been minted and both ids are valid during a deprecation window; superseded_with_redirect means consumers MUST follow the redirect; withdrawn_with_replacement means the original slot is no longer addressable and the replacement slot id is canonical. See contract §7.

denial_reason.description: Closed denial-reason set publishers MUST emit on non-conformance. See contract §10.

covered.description: Whether this slot ships an asset for this platform variant. Uncovered variants are declared coverage gaps; consumers MUST resolve uncovered variants against the universal asset_metaphor_ref or deny.

asset_metaphor_ref.description: Opaque ref to the canonical metaphor for this platform variant. Raw asset bytes never appear here; the ref points into the icon registry.

platform_native_alias.description: Optional short alias naming the native platform metaphor this variant honours (e.g. 'macos:trash', 'windows:recycle_bin').

platform_variant_coverage_record.description: One row per platform-variant coverage entry. The set is enumerable so reviewers can inspect platform parity rather than visually diff a screenshot.

predecessor_slot_id_refs.description: Opaque slot ids this slot supersedes. Empty for migration_state_class = frozen; non-empty for migrating_with_alias / superseded_with_redirect / withdrawn_with_replacement.

successor_slot_id_refs.description: Opaque slot ids that succeed this slot. Empty for migration_state_class = frozen; non-empty for migrating_with_alias / superseded_with_redirect / withdrawn_with_replacement when the slot has been replaced.

migration_decision_row_ref.description: Opaque pin to the decision row authorising this migration. Required (non-null) for safety-critical slots whose metaphor changes; optional otherwise.

migration_notes.description: Short note explaining what changed across the migration and why the change preserves (or explicitly redefines) the slot's meaning.

migration_record.description: One migration record per slot. Safety-critical slots whose metaphor changes MUST cite a migration_decision_row_ref.

slot_id.description: Opaque, stable slot id (e.g. 'icon-slot:command.save', 'icon-slot:trust.policy_locked', 'illustration-slot:empty.start_center'). Frozen on first publication; rename = breaking.

slot_revision_ref.description: Opaque revision id for this slot emission. Multiple revisions of the same slot_id share the slot_id but mint distinct revision refs.

platform_variant_coverage.description: Coverage matrix across the closed platform-variant set. A slot that ships one universal glyph emits exactly one row with platform_variant_class = not_applicable_universal; a slot with per-platform variants emits one row per platform plus an optional universal row used as a fallback.

default_metaphor_ref.description: Opaque ref to the canonical metaphor in the icon registry. Raw asset bytes never appear here; consumers resolve the ref through the icon registry.

command_id_ref.description: Opaque pin to the canonical command_id this icon represents. Required (non-null) when slot_kind_class = command_canonical.

semantic_status_class_ref.description: Required (non-null) when slot_kind_class = status_semantic; null otherwise.

trust_visual_state_class_ref.description: Required (non-null) when slot_kind_class = trust_state; null otherwise.

accessible_text_required.description: Whether consumers MUST pair this slot with an accessible text equivalent (label, tooltip, or aria-label). Frozen true for command_canonical, status_semantic, and trust_state slots. Decorative-only illustrations MAY set this to false.

non_color_cues_required.description: Frozen at true: every icon slot conveying state MUST be paired with shape, border, icon, or text. Reduced opacity or hue alone is non-conforming.

allOf.description: command_canonical slots MUST carry a non-null command_id_ref.
status_semantic slots MUST carry a non-null semantic_status_class_ref.
trust_state slots MUST carry a non-null trust_visual_state_class_ref and MUST be host_owned_no_override.
Safety-critical slots MUST cite a migration_decision_row_ref when their migration_state_class is anything other than frozen.
directional_chevron and directional_arrow slots MUST carry directionality_class = mirror_in_rtl.
Illustration slots MUST NOT serve safety-critical flows (illustrations are reserved for onboarding, empty states, and documentation per the design-system style guide).

icon_slot_map_record.description: One icon-slot or illustration-slot map record. See docs/ux/theme_and_visual_asset_contract.md §1.2 / §7.
